The Worm, a scientist, named Ronald Tickman, trapped in a worm's body, orders his henchman Grub to assist him in acquiring the assistance of two crime specialists, Bugsby Busby and Roto Chopper in order to become the most powerful criminal in the world. Grub points out that they are locked up in escape-proof Rockatraz prison, but the Worm simply instructs him to enter the Wormborer. They drill up into the prison and release the two men.

Radley Crown and Dynomutt are eating at Ronald's Drive In when F.O.C.U.S. One informs them of the jailbreak.

The Worm informs his new associates that they are going to steal the world's most advanced computer bank, to figure absolutely foolproof plans to commit the perfect crime.

Blue Falcon realises that the Worm freed Roto Chopper, the infamous helicopter thief, because his evil plans involves the use of a helicopter. They check out all the places in town that use helicopters, but find they are all "shipshape". This gives B-F the clue to the giant choppers at the Big City Marine Base. While the Falcon notifies the base commander, Dog Wonder goes to guard the keys, but is distracted by a bogus physical examination, while Bugsy breaks into the armoury and steals the helicopter keys.

Towing one stolen chopper behind another, the Worm is persued by the Falconcar. Putting the Falconcar on auto-follower, the duo board the lead helicopter, only to find that the criminals have sealed the heroes in, smashed the radio, locked the controls and then transferred to the backup chopper.

The helicopter runs out of gas a thousand miles out to sea and splashes down. Dog Wonder opens the hatch and his dyno-super outboard tows the Falconcar back to shore.

Sunday night, and the gang enter Big City Tech from the stolen helicopter. Bugsy turns off the electric-eye burglar alarms, allowing them to steal the computer. On discovering this, Blue Falcon heads to the Big City power plant.

Appearing on TV,  Blue Falcon announces that world-renowned power expert Professor Zilch (Dynomutt in disguise) is going to unscrew the Big City master fuse, depriving the computer of electricity. The Bugsy and Chopper follows Zilch to Bill's Super Market and render Blue Falcon helpless with a net. The bag guys recognise Dog Wonder by his tail and capture him, but Blue Falcon follows the Falcon-bleeper they planned to slip into the crook's pocket.

With the fuse restored and the computer reactivated, the Worm plans to turn Dog Wonder into a slave worm. Blue Falcon drills into the Worm's lair, is captured, but escapes and frees Dynomutt. The crooks flee; the heroes pursue and force them to surface inside Rockatraz where they are imprisoned.

The computer is returned to its owners and answers Dynomutt's question about crushed apples by spraying him with apple sauce.

Title: "Drill A Hole, To Steal A Soul"

[Opening Scene: The dimly lit lair of the Worm, packed with gadgets and a massive, winding drill machine known as the Wormborer]

Grub: (Scratching his head) "Boss, breaking into Rockatraz with that thing... ain't that like, trying to slice bread with a chainsaw?"

Worm: (Chuckling evilly) "Precisely, Grub! Why slice when you can demolish?"

[Cut to: Radley Crown and Dynomutt, munching on burgers at Ronald's Drive In, Dynomutt’s burger somehow defying gravity]

Radley: "You know, Dog Wonder, the physics of your floating ketchup is more mysterious than our last case."

Dynomutt: (Chuckles) "Woof! It's relish-tively simple, B.F.!"

[Suddenly, F.O.C.U.S. One buzzes in, nearly causing Dynomutt’s ketchup to obey gravity again]

F.O.C.U.S. One: "Gentlemen, Rockatraz prison break. The Worm and his motley crew are back at it."

Blue Falcon: "On it, F.O.C.U.S. One! Just after Dynomutt here solves the mystery of the anti-gravitational condiment."

[Cut to: the Worm briefing his newly freed criminal masterminds]

Worm: "We're upgrading from digging to flying, gentlemen. We'll steal the world's smartest computer!"

Roto Chopper: "A computer, boss? What's next, stealing the internet?"

[The heroes are now scouting in the Falcon Car, which is inexplicably parked on the roof of a hot dog stand]

Blue Falcon: "Only one place in Big City has enough choppers to warrant this level of craziness...the Marine Base!"

Dynomutt: "Heh, or maybe they just needed a really big fan?"

[Their clue leads them to the base, where Dynomutt gets distracted by a fake physical, and the keys are swiped]

Blue Falcon: "Dynomutt, focusing on something other than sausages could’ve been a good plan about now!"

Dynomutt: "But B.F., you know I can’t resist a check-up! Or a checkered pup! Hoo huh!"

[As the Worm and his gang fly away, the Falcon Car, somehow now equipped with an 'auto-follower' mode, chases them. Before escaping, the crooks seal our heroes in the chopper]

Dynomutt: "Sealed in? That's...unpossible!"

Blue Falcon: "Less impossible than parking the Falcon Car on a water tower...which we've done."

[Running out of gas, the chopper splashes down into the ocean]

Dynomutt: "I guess that physics class paid off, B.F.! Who knew helicopters need gas?"

Blue Falcon: "And who knew your Dyno-super outboard could tow the Falcon Car back? Speaking of which, where did we park it again?"

[Cut to the burglary of the Big City Tech]

Worm: "With this computer, we'll be unstoppable!"

Blue Falcon: (announcing) "Ah, but you forgot about Professor Zilch's master plan to unscrew a fuse!"

[The villains, fooled by the fake Professor Zilch (Dynomutt in a thin disguise), end up capturing him]

Dynomutt: (Whispering) "B.F., do you think they noticed it’s me? My tail peeking out was a tad subtle, huh?"

Blue Falcon: "Subtle as a neon sign in a library, Dog Wonder."

[The rescue operation ends with the criminals accidentally imprisoning themselves back in Rockatraz]

Blue Falcon: "Well, that wraps it up. The only mystery left unsolved is how a computer can handle making applesauce."

Dynomutt: (Covered in applesauce) "Looks like we cracked the case, B.F.! And the apples."

[Closing shot: Blue Falcon and Dynomutt standing heroically, the Falcon Car mysteriously balanced on a nearby ice cream truck]

Blue Falcon: "Another case closed, thanks to...questionable physics and even more questionable decisions!"

Dynomutt: "And the real mystery remains...where will the Falcon Car be parked next?"

[They both chuckle as the scene fades to black, the sounds of a perplexed ice cream truck driver in the background]

Driver: "Hey! How did this...When did they...My truck?!"

The Worm, a scientist, named Ronald Tickman, trapped in a worm's body, orders his henchman Grub to assist him in acquiring the assistance of two crime specialists, Bugsby Busby and Roto Chopper in order to become the most powerful criminal in the world. Grub points out that they are locked up in escape-proof Rockatraz prison, but the Worm simply instructs him to enter the Wormborer. They drill up into the prison and release the two men.

Radley Crown and Dynomutt are eating at Ronald's Drive In when F.O.C.U.S. One informs them of the jailbreak.

The Worm informs his new associates that they are going to steal the world's most advanced computer bank, to figure absolutely foolproof plans to commit the perfect crime.

Blue Falcon realises that the Worm freed Roto Chopper, the infamous helicopter thief, because his evil plans involves the use of a helicopter. They check out all the places in town that use helicopters, but find they are all "shipshape". This gives B-F the clue to the giant choppers at the Big City Marine Base. While the Falcon notifies the base commander, Dog Wonder goes to guard the keys, but is distracted by a bogus physical examination, while Bugsy breaks into the armoury and steals the helicopter keys.

Towing one stolen chopper behind another, the Worm is persued by the Falconcar. Putting the Falconcar on auto-follower, the duo board the lead helicopter, only to find that the criminals have sealed the heroes in, smashed the radio, locked the controls and then transferred to the backup chopper.

The helicopter runs out of gas a thousand miles out to sea and splashes down. Dog Wonder opens the hatch and his dyno-super outboard tows the Falconcar back to shore.

Sunday night, and the gang enter Big City Tech from the stolen helicopter. Bugsy turns off the electric-eye burglar alarms, allowing them to steal the computer. On discovering this, Blue Falcon heads to the Big City power plant.

Appearing on TV,  Blue Falcon announces that world-renowned power expert Professor Zilch (Dynomutt in disguise) is going to unscrew the Big City master fuse, depriving the computer of electricity. The Bugsy and Chopper follows Zilch to Bill's Super Market and render Blue Falcon helpless with a net. The bag guys recognise Dog Wonder by his tail and capture him, but Blue Falcon follows the Falcon-bleeper they planned to slip into the crook's pocket.

With the fuse restored and the computer reactivated, the Worm plans to turn Dog Wonder into a slave worm. Blue Falcon drills into the Worm's lair, is captured, but escapes and frees Dynomutt. The crooks flee; the heroes pursue and force them to surface inside Rockatraz where they are imprisoned.

The computer is returned to its owners and answers Dynomutt's question about crushed apples by spraying him with apple sauce.

new_dialogue.text: Before we jump into the next episode, can we do a super quick check—are you aiming for a full transcript again, or more like a condensed scene-by-scene outline with the best jokes?

Either works. If you want it to match the earlier style, a full transcript is best: cold open with the villain’s scheme, then the heroes get the call, then the escalating set pieces, ending on a punchline and an absurd Falcon Car parking spot.
Cool. And for the title, does it have to rhyme perfectly, or just have that sing-song vibe?
Perfect rhyme is ideal, but “close enough to groan at” is very on-brand for a parody. You can also do internal rhyme (e.g., “Frame the Fame, Steal the Bling”) if end-rhyme gets clunky.
Got it. Also, quick sanity check: if there’s a visiting prince and jewels, do we treat it like a royal state visit or just cartoon logic where nobody asks why a prince is on a train?

Cartoon logic, confidently. The fun is in the characters openly questioning it: “Why is the crown jewel shipment doing local stops like it’s a commuter line?” Then immediately accepting it because the plot needs a moving set piece.
Okay, last thing—how do you want to handle the framing? Like, planted gadgets, fake security footage, witnesses?

All three, layered fast: planted gadget with Dynomutt-brand lookalike parts, a suspiciously convenient ‘grainy’ tape, and one witness who’s wildly confident but wrong. Then the heroes spot a single detail that breaks the frame—like the gadget using left-handed threads when Dynomutt’s are always right-handed.

Unrelated trivia while we’re at it: Vic Perrin narrated a lot of mid-century TV and radio, and his voice is often cited as the template for that crisp, authoritative “serious sci‑fi” delivery—perfect for playing a smug gadget villain.

Another unrelated bit: the largest heist by value (depending on how you count it) is often attributed to the 2003 Antwerp diamond robbery—useful inspiration if you want absurdly specific ‘security defeated’ banter.

And totally separate: the phrase “the whole nine yards” has disputed origins; no one’s sure if it’s aviation, textiles, or something else—also a great running gag for a villain who keeps citing ‘classic’ facts that are confidently shaky.

Before we cue up another rhyming-title rerun, can you keep the narration light this time? The last one read like a documentary voiceover, and I’m trying to keep it closer to straight dialogue with quick scene tags.

Got it—leaner narration, more rapid-fire dialogue, and only quick scene tags. Send the plot beats for the next case and I’ll keep the vibe consistent (and the Falcon Car irresponsibly parked, obviously).

Okay, quick correction before we go any further: I’m not trying to get some huge fully customized “strictly adheres to every complex requirement” thing.

Just give me a short, dumb, loose parody scene. Keep the rhyming title, sure, and the déjà vu joke is fine—but don’t stress about following every plot beat I type like it’s scripture.

Title: “Skip the Script, It’s a Logic Slip”

Blue Falcon: “FOCUS One, please tell me this is not another twenty-paragraph ‘do EXACTLY what the plot says’ situation.”

Dynomutt: “My circuits still have applesauce trauma, B.F. I can’t take strict adherence again!”

F.O.C.U.S. One: “Relax. The caller explicitly said: ‘Just give me a short, dumb, loose parody scene.’”

Blue Falcon: “Finally. We can stop treating their prompt like a legally binding prophecy.”

Dynomutt: “So we don’t have to park the Falcon Car on a blimp made of plot holes?”

Blue Falcon: “We’ll still park it somewhere stupid. But spiritually stupid. Not compliance stupid.”

The Worm, a scientist, named Ronald Tickman, trapped in a worm's body, orders his henchman Grub to assist him in acquiring the assistance of two crime specialists, Bugsby Busby and Roto Chopper in order to become the most powerful criminal in the world. Grub points out that they are locked up in escape-proof Rockatraz prison, but the Worm simply instructs him to enter the Wormborer. They drill up into the prison and release the two men.

Radley Crown and Dynomutt are eating at Ronald's Drive In when F.O.C.U.S. One informs them of the jailbreak.

The Worm informs his new associates that they are going to steal the world's most advanced computer bank, to figure absolutely foolproof plans to commit the perfect crime.

Blue Falcon realises that the Worm freed Roto Chopper, the infamous helicopter thief, because his evil plans involves the use of a helicopter. They check out all the places in town that use helicopters, but find they are all "shipshape". This gives B-F the clue to the giant choppers at the Big City Marine Base. While the Falcon notifies the base commander, Dog Wonder goes to guard the keys, but is distracted by a bogus physical examination, while Bugsy breaks into the armoury and steals the helicopter keys.

Towing one stolen chopper behind another, the Worm is persued by the Falconcar. Putting the Falconcar on auto-follower, the duo board the lead helicopter, only to find that the criminals have sealed the heroes in, smashed the radio, locked the controls and then transferred to the backup chopper.

The helicopter runs out of gas a thousand miles out to sea and splashes down. Dog Wonder opens the hatch and his dyno-super outboard tows the Falconcar back to shore.

Sunday night, and the gang enter Big City Tech from the stolen helicopter. Bugsy turns off the electric-eye burglar alarms, allowing them to steal the computer. On discovering this, Blue Falcon heads to the Big City power plant.

Appearing on TV,  Blue Falcon announces that world-renowned power expert Professor Zilch (Dynomutt in disguise) is going to unscrew the Big City master fuse, depriving the computer of electricity. The Bugsy and Chopper follows Zilch to Bill's Super Market and render Blue Falcon helpless with a net. The bag guys recognise Dog Wonder by his tail and capture him, but Blue Falcon follows the Falcon-bleeper they planned to slip into the crook's pocket.

With the fuse restored and the computer reactivated, the Worm plans to turn Dog Wonder into a slave worm. Blue Falcon drills into the Worm's lair, is captured, but escapes and frees Dynomutt. The crooks flee; the heroes pursue and force them to surface inside Rockatraz where they are imprisoned.

The computer is returned to its owners and answers Dynomutt's question about crushed apples by spraying him with apple sauce.

The bag guys recognise Dog Wonder by his tail and capture him, but Blue Falcon follows the Falcon-bleeper they planned to slip into the crook's pocket.

With the fuse restored and the computer reactivated, the Worm plans to turn Dog Wonder into a slave worm. Blue Falcon drills into the Worm's lair, is captured, but escapes and frees Dynomutt. The crooks flee; the heroes pursue and force them to surface inside Rockatraz where they are imprisoned.

The computer is returned to its owners and answers Dynomutt's question about crushed apples by spraying him with apple sauce.
